2606.31522.title: FinPersona-Bench: A Benchmark for Longitudinal Psychometric Stability of Autonomous Financial Agents
2606.31522.paper_summary: Large Language Models (LLMs) are increasingly deployed as autonomous financial agents initialized with explicit behavioral mandates such as "preserve capital" or "avoid speculative bets" that are meant to govern every decision throughout deployment. In practice, however, as market context accumulates over long horizons, these mandates gradually lose their behavioral influence, a phenomenon we formalize as Mandate Salience Decay (MSD). To measure MSD objectively, we introduce FinPersona-Bench, a simulation benchmark in which a synthetic market decouples observable price from hidden fundamental value, enabling falsifiable evaluation across three failure modes: trading without signal in calm markets, panic-selling during crashes, and ignoring fundamental value during speculative bubbles. Evaluating 18 leading frontier and open-source LLMs, each assigned one of three behavioral profiles ranging from strict capital preservation to aggressive growth, shows that MSD compounds over time and is model-dependent. In crash scenarios, the behavioral gap between static agents and those receiving periodic mandate re-grounding grows 4.4x from the first to the final quarter of the simulation. The effects of mandate re-grounding are not uniformly positive: it consistently helps conservative agents in low-signal markets but actively worsens behavior for aggressive agents in the same setting. These findings suggest that reliable long-horizon deployment requires selective, mandate-aware re-grounding based on agent profile and market regime.
2606.31522.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）越來越多地被部署為自主金融代理，這些代理以明確的行為任務初始化，例如「保護資本」或「避免投機性賭注」，這些任務旨在指導整個部署過程中的每一個決策。 然而，實際上，隨著市場背景在長時間內的累積，這些任務逐漸失去其行為影響力，這一現象我們正式定義為任務顯著性衰退（MSD）。 為了客觀地測量MSD，我們引入了FinPersona-Bench，一個模擬基準，其中合成市場將可觀察的價格與隱藏的基本價值解耦，從而實現對三種失敗模式的可證偽評估：在平靜市場中無信號交易、在崩盤期間恐慌拋售，以及在投機泡沫期間忽視基本價值。 評估18個領先的前沿和開源LLMs，每個模型被分配一種三種行為特徵中的一種，從嚴格的資本保護到積極的增長，顯示MSD隨時間的推移而累積，且依賴於模型。在崩盤場景中，靜態代理與定期重新調整任務的代理之間的行為差距從模擬的第一季度增長到最後一季度達到4.4倍。 任務重新調整的效果並非均為正面：它在低信號市場中始終幫助保守型代理，但在相同環境中卻會積極惡化激進型代理的行為。 這些發現表明，可靠的長期部署需要根據代理特徵和市場制度進行選擇性、任務意識的重新調整。
2606.31522.author: Muhammad Usman Safder et.al.
2606.31522.authors: Muhammad Usman Safder, Ayesha Gull, Rania Elbadry, Fan Zhang, Yankai Chen, Xueqing Peng,  Xue,  Liu, Preslav Nakov, Zhuohan Xie
